title: Health & Safety Vocabulary Pack
description: A vocabulary pack for newcomer ELL students in grades 3-5 focusing on essential health and safety terms, including a mini-book and three worksheets.

vocabulary.image_prompt: A child washing hands thoroughly with soap and water.
Small, cartoon-like microscopic organisms floating in the air or on surfaces.
A child with a scraped knee or a small cut, looking slightly hurt.
A red cross symbol on a white box, with a bandage and antiseptic wipe nearby.
An ambulance with flashing lights and a siren, or a fire truck.
A child wearing a protective helmet while riding a bicycle.
A child buckled safely into a car seat with a seatbelt across their chest.
An unfamiliar adult face with a question mark or a 'no' symbol indicating caution.
Pills in a child-proof bottle and a spoon with liquid medicine, handled by an adult.
A child sneezing near a cat, or a food item (like peanuts) with a 'no eating' symbol.
A plate filled with a variety of healthy foods: fruits, vegetables, and whole grains.
Children running, jumping, or playing active games outdoors.
A poster with simple icons representing safety rules, like 'Stop, Look, Listen' or 'Walk, Don't Run'.
A child speaking to a trusted adult (teacher or parent) about a problem or concern.

worksheets.instructions_en: Match the English word to its correct definition.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Une la palabra en inglés con su definición correcta.

options_en: Tiny living things that can make you sick.
Practices to stay clean and healthy.
A hard hat worn for protection.
A serious situation needing immediate action.
The process of getting food necessary for health and growth.
options_l2: Pequeños seres vivos que pueden enfermarte.
Prácticas para mantenerse limpio y saludable.
Un casco duro que se usa para protegerse.
Una situación grave que requiere acción inmediata.
El proceso de obtener alimentos necesarios para la salud y el crecimiento.

worksheets.instructions_en: Trace the health and safety words.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Traza las palabras de salud y seguridad.

worksheets.instructions_en: Color the pictures that show good health and safety practices.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Colorea los dibujos que muestran buenas prácticas de salud y seguridad.

mini_book.title: Staying Healthy and Safe / Manteniéndonos Sanos y Seguros

pages.text_en: We learn about Health and Safety to keep ourselves and others well. It's important to know how to take care of our bodies and stay safe in different places.
pages.text_l2: Aprendemos sobre Salud y Seguridad para mantenernos bien a nosotros y a los demás. Es importante saber cómo cuidar nuestros cuerpos y mantenernos seguros en diferentes lugares.
pages.image_prompt: we learn about health and safety to keep ourselves and

pages.text_en: Good **Hygiene**, like washing hands often, helps stop **Germs** from spreading. Cover your mouth when you cough or sneeze!
pages.text_l2: Una buena **Higiene**, como lavarse las manos a menudo, ayuda a evitar que los **Gérmenes** se propaguen. ¡Cúbrete la boca al toser o estornudar!
pages.image_prompt: good **hygiene**, like washing hands often, helps stop

pages.text_en: If you get an **Injury**, like a scraped knee, tell an adult right away. They can help with **First Aid** to clean and cover the wound.
pages.text_l2: Si te haces una **Lesión**, como una raspadura en la rodilla, díselo a un adulto de inmediato. Ellos pueden ayudar con los **Primeros Auxilios** para limpiar y cubrir la herida.
pages.image_prompt: if you get an **injury**, like a scraped knee, tell an

pages.text_en: In an **Emergency**, like a fire or someone needing help, know who to call. Always wear a **Helmet** when riding a bike and a **Seatbelt** in the car.
pages.text_l2: En una **Emergencia**, como un incendio o alguien que necesita ayuda, sabe a quién llamar. Siempre usa un **Casco** al andar en bicicleta y un **Cinturón de seguridad** en el auto.
pages.image_prompt: in an **emergency**, like a fire or someone needing help,

pages.text_en: Never talk to or go with a **Stranger**. Only take **Medication** from a trusted adult, and tell them if you have an **Allergy** to certain foods or medicines.
pages.text_l2: Nunca hables ni vayas con un **Extraño**. Solo toma **Medicamentos** de un adulto de confianza, y diles si tienes una **Alergia** a ciertos alimentos o medicinas.
pages.image_prompt: never talk to or go with a **stranger**

pages.text_en: Eat healthy foods for good **Nutrition** and get plenty of **Exercise** every day. This keeps your body strong and healthy.
pages.text_l2: Come alimentos saludables para una buena **Nutrición** y haz mucho **Ejercicio** todos los días. Esto mantiene tu cuerpo fuerte y saludable.
pages.image_prompt: eat healthy foods for good **nutrition** and get plenty of

pages.text_en: Always follow **Safety Rules** at school, home, and outside. If you see something unsafe or someone needs help, always **Report** it to a trusted adult.
pages.text_l2: Siempre sigue las **Reglas de seguridad** en la escuela, en casa y afuera. Si ves algo inseguro o alguien necesita ayuda, siempre **Reporta**lo a un adulto de confianza.
pages.image_prompt: always follow **safety rules** at school, home, and outside

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to identify and use basic health and safety vocabulary in English and Spanish, and understand their importance in daily life.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce vocabulary using flashcards and realia. Read the mini-book aloud, then encourage students to read along or in small groups. Use worksheets for independent practice, reinforcement, and informal assessment. Facilitate discussions about personal safety and healthy habits.
materials_needed: Flashcards of vocabulary words with images
Markers or crayons for coloring worksheet
Scissors and glue for mini-book assembly
Chart paper or whiteboard for brainstorming and sentence building
Optional: Real-life examples of safety items (e.g., small helmet, first aid kit)
differentiation_tips: **Support:** Provide picture-to-word matching activities. Pair students with stronger English speakers. Allow students to draw their answers or use gestures. Pre-teach challenging vocabulary in smaller groups.

**Challenge:** Encourage students to write sentences or short paragraphs using the new vocabulary. Have them create their own 'Safety Rules' poster or role-play a scenario where they need to report an emergency or injury.

answers.1: Practices to stay clean and healthy. (Prácticas para mantenerse limpio y saludable.)
answers.2: Tiny living things that can make you sick. (Pequeños seres vivos que pueden enfermarte.)
answers.3: A serious situation needing immediate action. (Una situación grave que requiere acción inmediata.)
answers.4: A hard hat worn for protection. (Un casco duro que se usa para protegerse.)
answers.5: The process of getting food necessary for health and growth. (El proceso de obtener alimentos necesarios para la salud y el crecimiento.)

answer_key.answers: No specific answer key needed for tracing; completion indicates success.
All four images (child washing hands, child with helmet, plate with healthy food, child talking to teacher) should be colored as they represent positive health and safety practices.
